channel.title: TABI 窶・Discover Japan
channel.description: Your guide to the real Japan — travel, culture, and the things worth bringing home.

item.title: When to Take Your Shoes Off in Japan: The Complete Guide
item.description: The shoe rule in Japan is not arbitrary. It reflects a clear line between outside space and inside space that structures daily life. Here is where that line is drawn.

item.title: How to Behave in Public in Japan: The Unwritten Rules
item.description: Japanese public spaces operate on a set of unspoken rules that make them exceptionally pleasant. Most visitors don窶冲 know them. All of them are learnable in ten minutes.

item.title: Why You Should Never Tip in Japan 窶・and What to Do Instead
item.description: In Japan, the service is built into the price. Adding a tip doesn窶冲 reward the server 窶・it creates an awkward situation for both of you.

item.title: The Unwritten Rules of Japanese Trains 窶・Read This Before You Board
item.description: Japan窶冱 trains run on time to the minute and operate on a set of social rules that nobody posts on a sign. Here窶冱 what they are.

item.title: How to Visit a Japanese Temple or Shrine Without Being Accidentally Rude
item.description: Temples and shrines have different rules, different purposes, and different gods. Getting them confused is the starting mistake. Everything else follows from there.

item.title: The Tokyo Subway: How to Navigate It Without Getting Lost
item.description: Tokyo窶冱 train network is the most complex urban rail system in the world. It is also, once you understand the basics, extremely easy to use.

item.title: What You Can and Cannot Photograph in Japan
item.description: Japan is exceptionally photogenic. It also has specific places and situations where photography is restricted, disrespectful, or now actively prohibited. Knowing the difference matters.

item.title: The Only Japanese Phrases You Actually Need
item.description: Japanese is difficult. A handful of phrases isn窶冲. The ten expressions below will handle the majority of situations a visitor encounters, and they take about 20 minutes to learn.

item.title: Japan Still Runs on Cash 窶・Here窶冱 How Much to Bring and Where to Get It
item.description: Cards are increasingly accepted in Japan, but 窶亙ncreasingly窶・is not the same as 窶腕eliably.窶・Plan for cash and treat the card acceptance as a bonus.

item.title: SIM Card vs Pocket Wi-Fi in Japan: What Nobody Tells You Before You Choose
item.description: Both work. One is significantly more convenient. The answer has changed in the last three years and most travel articles haven窶冲 caught up.

item.title: Your First Ryokan: What to Expect, What to Do, and What Not to Wear
item.description: A traditional Japanese inn is not a hotel. The sooner you accept that, the better the stay.

item.title: Why January Is the Best Month to Visit Japan (Yes, Really)
item.description: No cherry blossoms. No 38-degree humidity. No crowds. January is when Japan stops performing for tourists and just exists.

item.title: The Convenience Store Is Japan's Greatest Culinary Achievement
item.description: Seven-Eleven in Japan is not Seven-Eleven anywhere else. This distinction matters and deserves your full attention.

item.title: Tokyo's Kissaten: The Coffee Shops That Refuse to Change
item.description: Somewhere between the third-wave cafés and the chain stores, Tokyo's old coffee shops have been quietly waiting.

item.title: Kanazawa: The City Most Japan Itineraries Skip — and Shouldn't
item.description: Three hours from Tokyo by bullet train. A fraction of Kyoto's crowds. Arguably better seafood. There is no good reason it's on so few itineraries.

item.title: Tsukiji Outer Market: Where to Actually Eat (and What to Skip)
item.description: The inner market moved to Toyosu in 2018. The outer market never moved and never needed to.

item.title: How to Eat at an Izakaya Without Looking Completely Lost
item.description: The curtain over the door. The laminated menu with pictures on maybe two pages. The small charge you didn't order. You'll be fine.

item.title: The IC Card Is the Best Thing in Japan. Here's How to Actually Use It.
item.description: You can survive Tokyo's train network without one. You just won't want to.

item.title: Japanese Whisky: What's Worth Buying and What's Just Marketing
item.description: The golden age is over but the whisky is still exceptional. You just need to know what you're actually looking at.

item.title: Naoshima Is Japan's Art Island — and Nobody Tells You How to Do It Right
item.description: It's a real island with real people living on it. The art just happens to be everywhere.
